轰.origin: Carts 车 rumbling together — it means a loud booming sound.

轴.origin: A cart 车 part — the axle that the wheels turn on.

轿.origin: A tall carriage 车 carried by people — a sedan-chair.

较.origin: A cart 车 set beside another to compare — it means to compare.

辅.origin: Something added to a cart 车 to help — it means to assist.

辆.origin: A measure word for vehicles like carts 车 and cars.

辈.origin: Rows like cart 车 wheels lining up — it means a generation.

辍.origin: A cart 车 that suddenly halts — it means to stop or quit.

辑.origin: To gather things onto a cart 车 — it means to collect or edit.

辕.origin: The long shaft 车 of a cart that an animal pulls.

sentence.en: The horse pulls the cart shaft.

辖.origin: A pin on a cart 车 wheel that controls it — it means to govern.

sentence.en: This place is under his jurisdiction.

辙.origin: The track 车 a cart leaves on the road as it moves.

sentence.en: There are wheel tracks on the road.

辣.origin: A sharp, burning taste — like hot peppers.

辨.origin: To separate two things with a knife 刂 — it means to tell apart.

辩.origin: An exchange of words 讠 between two sides — it means to debate.

辰.origin: An old word for the early morning hours and the stars.

sentence.en: There are stars in the sky.

辽.origin: To walk 辶 a long way — it means distant and far.

迁.origin: To walk 辶 to a new place — it means to move or shift.

迂.origin: To walk 辶 a winding, roundabout path.

迈.origin: To take big steps 辶 forward — it means to stride.

返.origin: To walk 辶 back the way you came — it means to return.

违.origin: To walk 辶 against the rules — it means to disobey.

迫.origin: To press 辶 someone hard — it means to force or compel.

迭.origin: To happen 辶 again and again — it means repeatedly.

述.origin: To walk 辶 through a story — it means to tell or state.

迷.origin: To lose your way 辶 — it means lost or fascinated.

迸.origin: To suddenly walk 辶 out together — it means to burst out.

退.origin: To walk 辶 backwards — it means to retreat or step back.

逃.origin: To run 辶 away from danger — it means to escape.

逆.origin: To walk 辶 the wrong way — it means to go against.

逊.origin: To step 辶 back and yield — it means humble or modest.

逐.origin: To walk 辶 after a pig 豕 — it means to chase, step by step.

递.origin: To walk 辶 and pass something along — it means to hand over.

逗.origin: To walk 辶 over to play — it means to tease or amuse.

逞.origin: To walk 辶 and show off — it means to flaunt or indulge.

sentence.en: He likes to show off his strength.

速.origin: To walk 辶 fast — it means quick or speedy.

逢.origin: To walk 辶 and run into someone — it means to meet.

逮.origin: To chase 辶 and reach — it means to catch or seize.

逼.origin: To press 辶 close — it means to force or pressure.

逾.origin: To walk 辶 over a line — it means to exceed or go beyond.

遂.origin: To walk 辶 on and get what you want — it means to fulfill.

sentence.en: May everything go smoothly for you.

遇.origin: To walk 辶 and come upon someone — it means to meet.

sentence.en: I ran into an old friend.

遏.origin: To walk 辶 up and block — it means to stop or curb.

遗.origin: To walk 辶 off and leave something valuable behind — to lose.

遛.origin: To walk 辶 around slowly — it means to stroll.

sentence.en: I'm going to walk the dog.

遭.origin: To walk 辶 into trouble — it means to encounter (something bad).

遮.origin: To walk 辶 in front and cover — it means to block or shield.

sentence.en: The big tree blocks the sunlight.

避.origin: To walk 辶 away from danger — it means to avoid.

邀.origin: To walk 辶 out and ask someone over — it means to invite.

邓.origin: A town 阝 by a place — used mostly as a family name.
